tickets.title: US001 (EDU Email Verification)
tickets.description:
As a: Student
I want to: register using my '.edu' email address
So that: the marketplace remains exclusive to verified campus members.

- [ ] Pass: Rejects non-.edu emails.
- [ ] Pass: Sends verification link to email.
- [ ] Pass: Unverified accounts cannot create listings, bid, or chat.

tickets.title: US002 (Safe Exchange Zone Selection)
tickets.description:
As a: Student
I want to: agree on a Safe Exchange Zone before chatting
So that: we can meet securely on campus.

- [ ] Pass: Shows list of pre-approved Safe Zones (e.g., Library).
- [ ] Pass: Requires both buyer and seller to select the same zone.
- [ ] Pass: Chat input unlocks only after mutual confirmation.

tickets.title: US003 (Barcode Scanner Listing)
tickets.description:
As a: Student
I want to: scan a textbook's barcode to auto-fill its details
So that: I can list items quickly without typing errors.

- [ ] Pass: Opens live camera viewfinder for scanning.
- [ ] Pass: Decodes ISBN-13 barcode within 2 seconds.
- [ ] Pass: Auto-populates Title and Author via catalog API.

tickets.title: US004 (Auction Listing)
tickets.description:
As a: Student
I want to: list items for auction with a starting price
So that: I can get competitive market prices for high-demand goods.

- [ ] Pass: Allows setting start price, increment, and end time.
- [ ] Pass: Rejects bids below minimum increment.
- [ ] Pass: Automatically ends auction at deadline and notifies winner.

analysis.ambiguity1: Barcode scanning request is vague. Scanning only gives raw ISBN numbers, not book metadata. It fails to specify which third-party API (e.g., Google Books) to use for decoding ISBNs into Titles/Authors, and lacks a fallback UI for unrecognised barcodes.
analysis.ambiguity2: The auction request is vague. It lacks rules for bidding duration, minimum increments, and bid retractions. Also, since in-app payments are forbidden by Legal, it doesn't specify how defaults are handled if a winning bidder refuses to pay.
analysis.conflictSolution:
Conflict: Student Union wants a 2% fee, but Legal forbids in-app payments.
Solution: Implement an indirect ledger. Sellers mark items as 'Sold', and the system logs a 2% debt on their account. Sellers pay the fee directly to the Union's Venmo and submit the receipt ID in-app. If unpaid after 14 days, their account is auto-suspended. This enforces fees without violating payment policies.
analysis.nfr1: The system must restrict registration exclusively to verified '.edu' emails. Users must confirm via a one-time link before accessing marketplace features.
analysis.nfr2: The system must force buyers and sellers to mutually agree on a designated 'Safe Exchange Zone' before unlocking the chat messaging interface.
analysis.nfr3: The mobile app must scan and decode a standard ISBN barcode in under 2.0 seconds in normal lighting to auto-fill book metadata.
